intro: Andruris australasica, also known as Kutaja or Indian Lythrum, is a plant species native to Australia and Asia, characterized by its pink or purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, treating fever, and alleviating symptoms of respiratory infections such as bronchitis and coughs. The bioactive constituents of Andruris australasica include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or a powder to be used in various herbal preparations, including teas and capsules, to promote overall health and well-being.
